column2: A company adds an approved outbound mail server. Receiving systems reject some of its messages because the server is not listed as an authorized sender for the domain. Which DNS-based email setting should be updated?
column3: The domain's SPF policy published in a DNS TXT record.
column4:
Why

An SPF policy published in DNS identifies which mail systems are authorized to send for a domain.

Exam Trap

DKIM validates a message signature. It does not define the domain's authorized sending-server list.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.12; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic HighYield SPF DNS EmailSecurity Source::Messer-v170

column2: A receiving mail system must verify the digital signature on a message by retrieving the sender domain's public key from DNS. Which mechanism provides this verification?
column4:
Why

DKIM signs outgoing mail, while the matching public key is published in a DNS TXT record for receiving systems to use.

Exam Trap

SPF evaluates whether a sending server is authorized; DKIM evaluates the message's digital signature.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.12; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic HighYield DKIM DNS EmailSecurity Source::Messer-v170

column2: A domain administrator wants receiving mail systems to follow a published policy when messages fail SPF or DKIM checks and to send compliance reports. Which mechanism should the administrator configure?
column4:
Why

DMARC adds handling policy and reporting around SPF and DKIM results.

Exam Trap

SPF and DKIM supply authentication evidence. DMARC tells receivers what policy to apply when that evidence does not validate.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.12; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic HighYield DMARC DNS EmailSecurity Source::Messer-v170

column2: A network printer should always receive the same IP address, but the administrator wants to manage the assignment centrally on the DHCP server. Which DHCP setting should be configured?
column3: A DHCP reservation for the printer's MAC address.
column4:
Why

A reservation lets DHCP consistently offer a selected address to a known device.

Real Help Desk

Centralizing the assignment avoids manually maintaining the IP configuration on the printer itself.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.13; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic HighYield DHCP Reservation Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Several infrastructure devices use manually configured addresses that fall inside a DHCP scope. Which setting prevents DHCP from offering those addresses to clients?
column4:
Why

An exclusion removes selected addresses from the scope's dynamic assignment pool.

Exam Trap

A reservation assigns a selected address to a known client. An exclusion simply prevents DHCP from distributing an address.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.13; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic DHCP Exclusion Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Existing clients retain valid addresses, but newly connected clients cannot obtain a dynamic address. The DHCP service is running. What DHCP configuration should be checked first?
column3: Check whether the DHCP scope has an available address.
column4:
Why

The scope defines the pool available to that subnet. A running server cannot lease a new address from an exhausted pool.

Exam Trap

Service availability and scope capacity are separate checks; a healthy DHCP process does not guarantee usable addresses remain.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.13; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic HighYield DHCP Scope Troubleshooting Source::Messer-v170

column2: What DHCP concept allows a client to use an assigned IP address for a configured period and renew the assignment?
column4:
Why

Leases let DHCP manage address use over time instead of making every dynamic assignment permanent.

Tech Wisdom

The appropriate lease duration depends on the network; there is no universal value to memorize for this objective.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.13; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic DHCP Lease Source::Messer-v170

column2: Two departments use the same physical switching infrastructure but must be placed in separate logical broadcast domains. What should the network administrator configure?
column4:
Why

VLANs provide logical separation without requiring a different physical switch for every network group.

Exam Trap

A VLAN defines logical network membership; it is not merely a label applied to an unchanged broadcast domain.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.14; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic HighYield VLAN Segmentation Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A remote employee needs an encrypted connection from one laptop to the company network. Which VPN model fits this requirement?
column4:
Why

Client-to-site VPN software connects an individual remote device to an organization's VPN endpoint.

Exam Trap

Site-to-site VPNs connect networks rather than a single roaming client.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.15; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic VPN RemoteAccess Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Two branch-office networks need a persistent encrypted connection across the internet. Which VPN model should connect them?
column4:
Why

Site-to-site VPN endpoints connect one network to another, allowing devices at both locations to use the protected path.

Exam Trap

Use client-to-site when the endpoint is an individual remote device rather than another network.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.15; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.4
column9: A+::220-1201::2.4 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::CommonNetworkConfigurationConcepts Basic VPN SiteToSite Scenario Source::Messer-v170
